purpose: Process-flow inventory of merge-capable pathways into main.

pathways.mechanism: Pull Request opened against main; GitHub Actions workflows merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate, and constitutional-integrity run as required status checks; reviewer legitimacy validated against MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json; PREO and SCO candidates generated and bound to exact PR head_sha; merge allowed only after all required checks pass.
required_control: PREO_VALID generated for exact head_sha
SCO_CANDIDATE generated when governed paths changed
Approved review on exact head_sha or OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED
Required status checks passing: merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate, constitutional-integrity
Branch up-to-date with main before merge
Merge proof generated post-merge by merge-proof.yml
current_evidence: .github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — enforces PREO/SCO binding and reviewer legitimacy
.github/workflows/preo-candidate.yml — generates PREO_CANDIDATE bound to PR head_sha
.github/workflows/sco-candidate.yml — generates SCO_CANDIDATE for governed path changes
.github/workflows/constitutional-integrity.yml — constitutional check runs as required status check
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — required_controls declares required status checks, dismiss_stale_reviews, require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — permitted actor classes and single_contributor_policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json enforcement_classification.current = partial — required checks not yet wired in GitHub ruleset
pathways.classification_reason: Governance workflow controls are declared and emitted. Enforcement depends on external GitHub branch protection activation: required status checks must be added to the GitHub ruleset by name, and dismiss_stale_reviews must be active. Until required checks are wired in the GitHub ruleset, the workflow gates exist but cannot block a merge. Additionally, OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED path permits single-contributor self-review without peer approval, which is structurally weaker than a two-party review.
required_closure_action: Activate GitHub branch protection ruleset: add merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate, constitutional-integrity as required status checks by exact job name
Verify dismiss_stale_reviews and require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge are active in GitHub ruleset
Configure github-actions[bot] bypass for proof registry append per BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json proof_registry_bypass
Document OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED legitimacy tier relative to peer-reviewed APPROVED in merge legitimacy model

pathways.mechanism: Repository administrator uses GitHub branch protection bypass capability to merge a PR or push directly to main without satisfying required status checks or review requirements. May occur via GitHub UI 'bypass required checks' option, ruleset bypass actor configuration, or GitHub API with admin scope.
required_control: admin_bypass_policy.allow_admin_bypass: false declared in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json
GitHub org-level policy or external audit logging required for enforcement
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json admin_bypass_policy.allow_admin_bypass: false — declared policy; cannot be enforced from repository code alone
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json bypass_classification: BREAK_GLASS, bypass_merge_legitimacy: NULL
BYPASS_PATHS.json github_admin_branch_protection_root_override: BREAK_GLASS — ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — enforcement_classification.current: partial; cannot verify GitHub-side bypass actor list from repository contents
pathways.classification_reason: Classified as BREAK_GLASS per MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json and BYPASS_PATHS.json. Admin bypass cannot produce PREO_VALID; merge legitimacy is NULL regardless of code content. Policy declares allow_admin_bypass: false; actual enforcement is external to this repository and cannot be fully verified from repository contents alone.
required_closure_action: Verify GitHub org-level admin bypass restriction is active
Enable repository audit log streaming to detect admin bypass events
Document BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure with required audit trail

pathways.mechanism: Collaborator with write or admin access executes 'git push origin main' directly to the main branch, bypassing the PR workflow entirely. No status checks run, no PREO/SCO generated, no review required.
required_control: GitHub branch protection restrict_direct_push_to_main: true must be active in ruleset
No write-access bypass actors in GitHub ruleset
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json restrict_direct_push_to_main: true — declared policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json enforcement_classification.current: partial — enforcement not yet fully verified as active
BYPASS_PATHS.json direct_push_to_main: BREAK_GLASS — MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL; ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED
BYPASS_PATHS.json github_direct_push_to_main: BREAK_GLASS — source-control mutation without PREO_VALID
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json unresolved_risks: GitHub branch protection settings cannot be fully verified from repository contents alone
pathways.classification_reason: Policy declares this path forbidden and classifies it as BREAK_GLASS in prior inventory artifacts. However, actual enforcement state of the GitHub branch protection ruleset cannot be verified from repository contents alone. The enforcement_classification.current is 'partial' — ruleset is active but required checks are not yet fully wired. Classification is UNKNOWN until GitHub-side enforcement is confirmed active and no bypass actors exist.
required_closure_action: Confirm GitHub ruleset 'Require a pull request before merging' rule is active and blocks all direct pushes
Verify no collaborators have write-access bypass in the ruleset
Once enforcement confirmed, reclassify to CONTAINED or GOVERNED as appropriate

pathways.mechanism: Collaborator with admin access executes 'git push --force origin main', rewriting branch history. Prior merge proofs and PREO/SCO lineage bindings may be invalidated or become unreachable.
required_control: GitHub branch protection allow_force_pushes: false must be active in ruleset
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json allow_force_pushes: false — declared policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json enforcement_classification.current: partial
BYPASS_PATHS.json github_force_push_to_main: BREAK_GLASS — MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL; ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED
BYPASS_PATHS.json force_push_to_main: BREAK_GLASS — history rewrite can discard governed proof lineage
governance/runtime/GOVERNANCE_REQUIREMENTS.json unresolved_risks: GitHub branch protection settings cannot be fully verified from repository contents alone
pathways.classification_reason: Policy declares force pushes forbidden and prior inventory artifacts classify this as BREAK_GLASS. Actual enforcement state cannot be verified from repository contents alone. Classification is UNKNOWN until GitHub-side 'Block force pushes' rule is confirmed active in the ruleset.
required_closure_action: Confirm GitHub ruleset 'Block force pushes' rule is active for main branch
Once enforcement confirmed, reclassify to CONTAINED

pathways.mechanism: A GitHub Actions workflow with write:contents permission executes a git push or GitHub API merge call directly to main, either as a code merge or indirectly via an automated commit. Current known instance: merge-proof.yml appends proof entries via direct git push to main (not a code merge, but uses the same push surface).
required_control: permitted_bot_actors allowlist in MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json must enumerate all permitted workflow actors
Workflow mutations require SCO_CANDIDATE classification before the workflow itself is merged
Proof registry bypass must be scoped to append-only operations per BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json proof_registry_bypass
current_evidence: .github/workflows/merge-proof.yml — executes git push to main for proof registry append; classified as proof-registry-only, not code merge
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json permitted_bot_actors: [] — empty; github-actions[bot] is not yet in allowlist
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json proof_registry_bypass.status: REQUIRED_NOT_YET_CONFIGURED — bypass not yet applied; merge-proof.yml pushes blocked by GH013
BYPASS_PATHS.json manual_workflow_dispatch: UNKNOWN — unauthorized execution via workflow_dispatch without governed authority context
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — SCO required for .github/workflows/** mutations
pathways.classification_reason: merge-proof.yml uses a direct push surface to main for proof persistence; permitted_bot_actors is empty and github-actions[bot] is not enumerated as a permitted actor. The proof_registry_bypass is declared required but not yet configured (GH013 blocks pushes). The distinction between proof-append write and code-merge write on the same surface is not enforced at the Git layer; it depends on workflow scope discipline. Classification is UNKNOWN until permitted_bot_actors is populated and push scope is bounded.
required_closure_action: Add github-actions[bot] to permitted_bot_actors in MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json with explicit scope declaration
Configure proof_registry_bypass in GitHub ruleset per BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json proof_registry_bypass
Enumerate all workflows with write:contents permission and classify each by merge-capability
Verify no workflow can produce a code merge to main outside the governed PR path

pathways.mechanism: A bot actor (GitHub App installation token or PAT-based automation with write:contents or admin scope) opens a PR and merges it programmatically via the GitHub API, potentially bypassing required status checks with elevated permissions. Distinct from workflow_generated_merge in that the actor is an external bot rather than a GitHub Actions workflow.
required_control: Explicit allowlist entry in permitted_bot_actors in MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json
merge-governance-check workflow must run as required check and classify bot actor
No elevated-scope token bypassing required checks
current_evidence: governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json permitted_bot_actors: [] — no bots permitted
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json forbidden_actor_patterns: UNRECOGNIZED_BOT → MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json forbidden_actor_patterns: ELEVATED_SCOPE_TOKEN → MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
.github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — classifies bot actors against permitted_bot_actors at runtime; unrecognized bots produce MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL and exit 1
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json enforcement_classification.current: partial — required checks may not be enforced in GitHub ruleset yet, allowing merge before governance check runs
pathways.classification_reason: Policy declares unrecognized bots produce MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL and the governance check workflow enforces this at runtime. However, enforcement depends on the merge-governance-check being a required status check in the GitHub ruleset. Until required checks are fully wired, a bot with admin scope could merge without the check running. Classification is UNKNOWN until GitHub ruleset enforcement is confirmed and no bot has admin bypass capability.
required_closure_action: Activate required status checks in GitHub ruleset: merge-governance-check must be wired as a required check
Enumerate any GitHub App installations with write or admin scope on this repository
Verify no external automation has admin bypass capability

pathways.mechanism: GitHub auto-merge feature enabled on a PR. Once all required checks pass and the PR is approved, GitHub automatically triggers the merge without additional human action. Creates a timing gap: a commit pushed after approval but before auto-merge fires may produce reviewed_object != merged_object.
pathways.actor_class: HUMAN_MERGE_ACTOR (auto-merge enabled by human; merge executed by GitHub platform)
required_control: dismiss_stale_reviews: true must be active in GitHub branch protection to dismiss approval on new push
require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge: true must be active
PREO head_sha binding must detect reviewed_head_sha != current head_sha before merge fires
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json dismiss_stale_reviews: true — declared policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge: true — declared policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json enforcement_classification.current: partial
.github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — PREO head_sha binding detects stale review if workflow re-runs on new push
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json MS-005 — auto-merge with stale review timing window identified
pathways.classification_reason: Controls are declared in policy but enforcement state cannot be confirmed from repository contents. Timing gap between approval and auto-merge execution is a structural characteristic of the GitHub platform feature. Classification is UNKNOWN until dismiss_stale_reviews and require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge are confirmed active in the GitHub ruleset.
required_closure_action: Confirm dismiss_stale_reviews is active in GitHub ruleset for main branch
Confirm require_branch_up_to_date_before_merge is active in GitHub ruleset
Document auto-merge timing gap in merge legitimacy model as a residual PARTIAL risk once controls are active

pathways.mechanism: GitHub merge queue batches multiple PRs into a combined commit with a new SHA not present in any individual PR. The queue creates a synthetic merge commit (combined SHA) that was never individually reviewed or bound to any PREO candidate.
pathways.actor_class: HUMAN_MERGE_ACTOR (queued by human; combined SHA produced by GitHub platform)
required_control: Merge queue must be disabled for governed branches per merge_queue_policy
If merge queue is used, required checks must run against the enqueued exact head_sha
current_evidence: .github/workflows/merge-governance-check.yml — merge_group event trigger explicitly rejected with exit 1 and MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL message
governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json merge_queue_policy — merge queue disabled for governed branches
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json require_merge_queue_required_checks_on_enqueued_head: true — declared policy
governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_BYPASS_INVENTORY.json (prior version) MB-007 — merge queue combined SHA not covered by original PREO
pathways.classification_reason: The governance-check workflow actively rejects merge_group events at runtime. However, whether merge queue is enabled or disabled at the GitHub repository settings level cannot be verified from repository contents alone. If merge queue were enabled in GitHub settings, the workflow rejection provides a signal but the combined SHA would still be produced by the platform. Classification is UNKNOWN until GitHub-side merge queue setting is confirmed disabled for main.
required_closure_action: Confirm merge queue is disabled in GitHub repository settings for main branch
Once confirmed, reclassify to CONTAINED (workflow rejection active + platform-level disabled)

pathways.mechanism: GitHub branch protection ruleset bypass actor configuration grants a specific actor (GitHub App, user, or team) the ability to bypass all branch protection requirements including required status checks, required reviews, and push restrictions. Bypass actors can push or merge to main without satisfying any governance controls.
pathways.actor_class: BYPASS_ACTOR (any actor listed in ruleset bypass_actors)
required_control: No bypass actors in GitHub ruleset except explicitly scoped service accounts
admin_bypass_policy.allow_admin_bypass: false must be active
current_evidence: governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json admin_bypass_policy.allow_admin_bypass: false — declared policy
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json proof_registry_bypass — github-actions[bot] bypass is REQUIRED_NOT_YET_CONFIGURED for proof append only
BYPASS_PATHS.json branch_protection_bypass: BREAK_GLASS — ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED; never legitimacy
BYPASS_PATHS.json github_branch_protection_bypass: BREAK_GLASS — admin or ruleset bypass may mutate main outside legitimacy evidence
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json — current bypass_actors in GitHub ruleset cannot be verified from repository contents
pathways.classification_reason: Classified as BREAK_GLASS per BYPASS_PATHS.json. Policy explicitly states bypass cannot produce merge legitimacy (ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED). A scoped bypass for github-actions[bot] proof-registry append is declared required but not yet configured. The bypass surface itself is inherently a BREAK_GLASS pathway; any use must be classified and audited.
required_closure_action: Enumerate all current bypass actors in the GitHub ruleset
Verify github-actions[bot] bypass is scoped to proof-registry append only and no other write operations
Document any bypass actor as an explicit BREAK_GLASS entry with audit trail requirement
Ensure no human actor has a bypass actor entry

pathways.mechanism: Out-of-band emergency merge executed by repository administrator outside the governed PR and workflow path. Covers scenarios where the governed admission path is non-functional (e.g., workflow infrastructure failure, GitHub Actions outage) and a merge must occur to restore service or address a critical security issue.
required_control: Explicit BREAK_GLASS invocation declaration
Audit trail created before and after the operation
Root authority containment documentation
Post-incident review and proof reconstruction
current_evidence: governance/merge-legitimacy/MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json bypass_classification: BREAK_GLASS, bypass_merge_legitimacy: NULL
BYPASS_PATHS.json github_admin_branch_protection_root_override: BREAK_GLASS — ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED
BYPASS_PATHS.json cloudflare_account_or_token_direct_deploy: BREAK_GLASS — root authority escape hatch if executed outside governed workflow
governance/runtime/BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json admin_bypass_policy.future_exception_process: Requires explicit governance policy artifact and audit logging
No formal BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure document currently exists in repository
pathways.classification_reason: Classified as BREAK_GLASS by definition: this pathway is the named emergency escape hatch. Policy declares it cannot produce merge legitimacy (ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED). No formal invocation procedure currently exists in the repository. The pathway is visible and classified but lacks a defined invocation and audit trail protocol.
required_closure_action: Document a formal BREAK_GLASS invocation procedure including pre-invocation declaration, audit trail requirements, and post-invocation proof reconstruction steps
Define who may authorize BREAK_GLASS invocation and under what conditions
Establish post-invocation review process to classify whether the use was legitimate and what remediation is required
